summary: Theme Switching Visual Test: PARTIAL SUCCESS. The profile.tsx now uses dynamic theme colors (colors.background.primary, colors.text.primary/secondary/muted) from useTheme() hook. Theme selection UI works correctly with checkmarks moving between options. Theme IS internally applied - detected different background colors in inner divs when switching themes (Dark: rgb(26, 21, 32) = #1A1520, Light: rgb(254, 252, 255) = #FEFCFF). However, a parent wrapper div with hardcoded gray background rgb(242, 242, 242) is overlaying the themed content, preventing the visual theme change from being visible to users.

integration_issues.issue: A parent wrapper div (likely from SafeAreaProvider or react-native-web defaults) has hardcoded background color rgb(242, 242, 242) that overlays the correctly-themed SafeAreaView background. The theme system is working internally but the visual result is hidden.

test_results.login: PASS - Successfully logged in as demo.mom@truejoybirthing.com
test_results.profile_navigation: PASS - Navigated to Profile page via bottom nav
test_results.appearance_section_visible: PASS - Appearance section renders with all 3 options
test_results.theme_selection_ui: PASS - Checkmarks correctly move between selected options
test_results.theme_internal_application: PASS - Inner div shows correct themed colors (Dark: #1A1520, Light: #FEFCFF)
test_results.theme_visual_change: FAIL - Parent wrapper with gray background covers themed content

debug_findings.wrapper_div_bg: rgb(242, 242, 242) = #F2F2F2 (not in theme constants - likely react-native-web default)
debug_findings.root_cause: Multiple nested divs with first large div (idx 5) having hardcoded gray background that sits on top of/before the themed div (idx 7)

profile_tsx_dynamic_styles: Line 359: SafeAreaView correctly uses { backgroundColor: colors.background.primary }
Line 400: userName correctly uses { color: colors.text.primary }
Line 401: userEmail correctly uses { color: colors.text.secondary }
Line 402: tapToEdit correctly uses { color: colors.text.muted }
code_review.remaining_static_colors: Most other styles in profile.tsx still use static COLORS import from theme.ts (e.g., Card backgrounds, button colors, icon colors)

action_items: PRIORITY 1: Fix parent wrapper background - Either update SafeAreaProvider/root layout to use theme colors, or set body/html background via useEffect + theme context
PRIORITY 2: Consider adding CSS for web to override react-native-web defaults: body { background-color: var(--bg-primary) } with CSS variables updated by theme
PRIORITY 3: Update remaining static COLORS usages in profile.tsx to use theme.colors for complete dark mode support
critical_code_review_comments: The theme system infrastructure is correctly implemented - the issue is purely visual/CSS layering
SafeAreaProvider from react-native-safe-area-context may need style prop for background color
Root _layout.tsx line 112 sets contentStyle but react-native-web may add additional wrapper divs

success_rate.frontend: 75% - Theme system works internally, only visual display is blocked by parent wrapper

context_for_next_testing_agent: Theme system IS working correctly under the hood. The issue is a gray wrapper div (rgb(242, 242, 242)) overlaying the themed content. Debug showed inner div correctly applying #1A1520 (dark) and #FEFCFF (light) backgrounds. Need to trace where the gray wrapper comes from - likely SafeAreaProvider or react-native-web defaults. After fixing wrapper, theme visual switching should work immediately.
verified_features: Profile page uses useTheme() hook and colors object
Username/email text uses dynamic theme colors
Checkmark moves correctly on theme selection
Theme context correctly returns different colors for LIGHT vs DARK

partial_success_evidence.dark_mode_detected: Inner div shows rgb(26, 21, 32) which equals #1A1520 from DARK_COLORS.background.primary
partial_success_evidence.light_mode_detected: Inner div shows rgb(254, 252, 255) which equals #FEFCFF from LIGHT_COLORS.background.primary
